title: Think Tanks in America By Thomas Medvetz Chicago University Press. 2012. 324 pages. $32.50 cloth
abstract: Organizations like Brookings, the RAND Corporation and the American Enterprise Institute have come to be an important part of the political landscape in the United States—just how important, and how puzzling, was underscored most recently in December 2012, when South Carolina Republican Jim DeMint decided to leave his elected position in the Senate to accept a job at the Heritage Foundation. Discussions about think tanks have often followed rather predictable and predictably normative patterns and it is easy to say after reading Tom Medvetz's Think Tanks in America that these organizations have rather been lying around waiting for someone like him to bring the full array of sociological tools to bear on the analysis of their history and current role. Think Tanks in America is the kind of book that makes one realize in hindsight that it has had a place waiting for it in the space of possibilities for important books.
